title: Synapse — browser deep-learning lab
description: A deep-learning framework built from scratch on a real reverse-mode tensor autograd engine (no TensorFlow.js) — 30+ hand-derived ops including conv2d, max/avg pooling, LayerNorm, BatchNorm, dropout, GELU/SiLU, embedding, a full softmax Jacobian, and a fused BCE-with-logits + closed-form Gaussian KL, every one proven correct by a one-click engine self-test that gradchecks it (and a whole Transformer AND a whole VAE end-to-end) to ~1e-7. Four labs share the engine: a 2-D Playground (sketch an MLP, train it live on spirals/checkerboards with AdamW/Nesterov, schedules and clipping, and watch decision boundaries, neuron feature maps, train-vs-val curves and a live computation graph); a Vision lab that trains a from-scratch CNN on a fully procedural image dataset (handwritten-style digits 0–9 and shapes) with live filter/feature-map views, a confusion matrix and a draw-your-own pad; a Transformer · Attention lab that trains a from-scratch decoder-only GPT on procedural algorithmic tasks (copy/reverse/sort/add) and shows the live per-layer, per-head causal attention maps, greedy decoding coloured against ground truth, a type-your-own decoder with confidence bars, and a token-embedding PCA; a Generative · VAE lab that trains a from-scratch variational autoencoder (reparameterization trick + KL) on those glyphs and renders a live navigable 2-D latent manifold of synthesised digits, side-by-side reconstructions, a class-coloured latent-space scatter, samples drawn from the prior, latent interpolation between two glyphs, and a slider-driven latent explorer; and a Control · RL lab that trains a from-scratch policy-gradient agent (REINFORCE, REINFORCE+baseline, and advantage actor–critic with GAE) on two hand-written environments — CartPole with the real gym dynamics, and a GridWorld maze — and shows the live agent acting frame-by-frame, the episode-return + policy-entropy learning curves, the live action distribution, and either the CartPole policy phase-portrait or the GridWorld value heatmap with greedy-policy arrows (only two new engine ops — logSoftmax and gatherCols — were needed, both hand-derived and gradchecked alongside a whole-policy end-to-end check, for 39 self-tested ops in all). Save, load and share experiments via a URL link.
